title: Summer Training Program on Accelerated Computing and Gen AI
summary: The program aimed to cover the fundamentals of Deep Learning and the emerging field of Generative AI (GenAI) for text and image generation. The program is designed for third-year undergraduate students who are keen to build strong foundations and practical skills in HPC, AI, and GenAI and explore research or career opportunities in these fields.  This program also introduced participants to NVIDIA’s powerful platform for GPU-based parallel computing. It also introduced various modern concepts on Artificial Intelligence (AI).

title: Summer Training Program on Gen AI and Accelerated Computing
summary: The program aimed to cover the fundamentals of accelerated computing and Generative AI for text and image generation. The program was designed for third-year undergraduate students who are keen to build strong foundations and practical skills parallel programming models, including Open Multiprocessing (OpenMP), Message Passing Interface (MPI) and Compute Unified Device Architecture (CUDA).

title: Faculty Development Program on HPC and AI
summary: This FDP aimed to be a valuable learning experience for all participants, offering both theoretical insights and practical exposure to cutting-edge HPC technologies and their real-world applications in Artificial Intelligence. The Department of Computer Engineering at VIT Pune, through this initiative, continues its mission to foster academic excellence and technological advancement among the teaching and research community.

title: Faculty Development Program on High Performance Computing & AI
location: Yashwantrao Bhosale Institute of Technology, Sawantwadi
summary: To provide faculty members with comprehensive exposure to High Performance Computing (HPC) and Artificial Intelligence (AI), through expert lectures, hands-on sessions, and interdisciplinary discussions.

title: Faculty Development Program on HPC and AI
location: Sardar Patel Institute of Technology, Mumbai
summary: The FDP aimed to provide faculty members with knowledge and hands-on experience in cutting-edge technologies such as parallel programming, artificial intelligence, and computing architecture. The sessions were conducted by renowned experts from academia and industry.

title: Faculty Orientation Program and Inauguration of HPC Lab
location: Bharati Vidyapeeth College of Engineering, Pune
summary: The Bharati Vidyapeeth (Deemed to be University) College of Engineering, Pune BV(DU)COE, successfully hosted the inauguration of its newly established High-Performance Computing (HPC) Laboratory and a one-day faculty orientation program. The event was held under the National Supercomputing Mission (NSM) initiative, a project launched by the Government of India, in collaboration with the Centre for Development of Advanced Computing (C-DAC) Pune and Walchand College of Engineering (WCE), Sangli.

title: Faculty Development Program on Integrating HPC in Education and Research
location: Rayat Shikshan Sanstha’s, Karmaveer Bhaurao Patil College of Engineering, Satara
summary: The Faculty Orientation Program aimed to provide a platform for educators and researchers to gain insights into the transformative role of High Performance Computing (HPC) in academia and research. The program focused on emerging technologies such as Artificial Intelligence (AI) and their integration into educational and research domains.

title: Faculty Development Program on HPC and AI
location: Marathwada Mitra Mandal's College of Engineering, Pune
summary: The Faculty Development Program (FDP) on "High Performance Computing & Artificial Intelligence" was conducted from 17th March 2025 to 22nd March 2025. The FDP aimed to provide faculty members with knowledge and hands-on experience in cutting-edge technologies such as parallel programming, artificial intelligence, and computing architecture. The sessions were conducted by renowned experts from academia and industry.

title: Faculty Development in HPC
summary: The FDP is offered in association with CDAC, Pune under the National Supercomputing Mission (NSM) activity. The FDP was aimed as an introduction to those, looking at High Performance Computing from the perspective of researchers, academician and instructor with the hands-on from Industry experts and academicians.
